description_long: This is a tall, slender stone clock tower with a square base and a sharply pointed spire roof. The exterior is constructed from light gray stone blocks, with visible mortar lines and subtle weathering, giving it a historic and robust appearance. The upper section features arched belfry openings on each side, some with visible louvres, and a projecting cornice below the spire. The spire itself is steeply pitched and capped with a small finial. There are no visible clock faces on any side, and the tower is adorned with minimal decorative elements, focusing on clean lines and structural solidity. The base of the tower is slightly wider, with a few small architectural protrusions and what appears to be a doorway or entrance.
description: A tall, historic stone clock tower with a pointed spire roof, arched belfry openings, and a robust, weathered exterior. No visible clock faces or ornate decorations.
description_view_0: Front view shows the pointed spire, arched belfry openings, and a slightly protruding base with a possible entrance.
description_view_1: This side is brightly lit, highlighting the stone texture and the clean lines of the cornice and spire; no clock face is visible.
description_view_2: A slightly angled view showing the belfry arches and the robust stonework, with some architectural details at the base.
description_view_3: This view emphasizes the height and slenderness of the tower, with the spire and belfry arches clearly visible.
